values.name: SCROLL_HINT_BOTTOM_EDGE
values.summary: A preferred position for the node scrolled into view. Bottom edge of the scroll container.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_BOTTOM_RIGHT
values.summary: A preferred position for the node scrolled into view. Bottom-right edge of the scroll container.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_LEFT_EDGE
values.summary: A preferred position for the node scrolled into view. Left edge of the scroll container.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_RIGHT_EDGE
values.summary: A preferred position for the node scrolled into view. Right edge of the scroll container.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_TOP_EDGE
values.summary: A preferred position for the node scrolled into view. Top edge of the scroll container.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_TOP_LEFT
values.summary: A preferred position for the node scrolled into view. Top-left edge of the scroll container.

values.name: SCROLL_UNIT_ITEM
values.summary: The amount by which to scroll. A single item of a list, line of text.

values.name: SCROLL_UNIT_PAGE
values.summary: The amount by which to scroll. A single page.

values.name: CURSOR_ARROW
values.summary: Arrow cursor shape. This is the default when not pointing anything that overrides the mouse cursor, such as a [LineEdit] or [TextEdit].

values.name: CURSOR_BDIAGSIZE
values.summary: Secondary diagonal resize cursor (top-right/bottom-left). Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be resized on both axes at once using the mouse.

values.name: CURSOR_BUSY
values.summary: Wait cursor. On most cursor themes, this [i]replaces[/i] the arrow with a spinning icon. Intended to be used for blocking operations (when the user can't do anything else at the moment). See also [constant CURSOR_WAIT].

values.name: CURSOR_CAN_DROP
values.summary: "Can drop" cursor. This is displayed during drag-and-drop operations if hovering over a [Control] that can accept the drag-and-drop event. On most cursor themes, this displays a dragging hand with an arrow symbol besides it. See also [constant CURSOR_DRAG].

values.name: CURSOR_CROSS
values.summary: Crosshair cursor. This is intended to be displayed when the user needs precise aim over an element, such as a rectangle selection tool or a color picker.

values.name: CURSOR_DRAG
values.summary: Dragging hand cursor. This is displayed during drag-and-drop operations. See also [constant CURSOR_CAN_DROP].

values.name: CURSOR_FDIAGSIZE
values.summary: Main diagonal resize cursor (top-left/bottom-right). Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be resized on both axes at once using the mouse.

values.name: CURSOR_FORBIDDEN
values.summary: Forbidden cursor. This is displayed during drag-and-drop operations if the hovered [Control] can't accept the drag-and-drop event.

values.name: CURSOR_HELP
values.summary: Help cursor. On most cursor themes, this displays a question mark icon instead of the mouse cursor. Intended to be used when the user has requested help on the next element that will be clicked.

values.name: CURSOR_HSIZE
values.summary: Horizontal resize cursor. Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be horizontally resized using the mouse. See also [constant CURSOR_HSPLIT].

values.name: CURSOR_HSPLIT
values.summary: Horizontal split cursor. This is displayed when hovering a [Control] with splits that can be horizontally resized using the mouse, such as [HSplitContainer]. On some cursor themes, this cursor may have the same appearance as [constant CURSOR_HSIZE].

values.name: CURSOR_IBEAM
values.summary: I-beam cursor shape. This is used by default when hovering a control that accepts text input, such as [LineEdit] or [TextEdit].

values.name: CURSOR_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum CursorShape] enum.

values.name: CURSOR_MOVE
values.summary: Move cursor. Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be moved using the mouse.

values.name: CURSOR_POINTING_HAND
values.summary: Pointing hand cursor shape. This is used by default when hovering a [LinkButton] or a URL tag in a [RichTextLabel].

values.name: CURSOR_VSIZE
values.summary: Vertical resize cursor. Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be vertically resized using the mouse. See also [constant CURSOR_VSPLIT].

values.name: CURSOR_VSPLIT
values.summary: Vertical split cursor. This is displayed when hovering a [Control] with splits that can be vertically resized using the mouse, such as [VSplitContainer]. On some cursor themes, this cursor may have the same appearance as [constant CURSOR_VSIZE].

values.name: CURSOR_WAIT
values.summary: Wait cursor. On most cursor themes, this displays a spinning icon [i]besides[/i] the arrow. Intended to be used for non-blocking operations (when the user can do something else at the moment). See also [constant CURSOR_BUSY].

values.name: FEATURE_ACCESSIBILITY_SCREEN_READER
values.summary: Display server supports interaction with screen reader or Braille display. [b]Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_CLIPBOARD
values.summary: Display server supports setting and getting clipboard data. See also [constant FEATURE_CLIPBOARD_PRIMARY]. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, iOS, Web[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_CLIPBOARD_PRIMARY
values.summary: Display server supports Primary clipboard can be used. This is a different clipboard from [constant FEATURE_CLIPBOARD]. [b]Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_CURSOR_SHAPE
values.summary: Display server supports setting the mouse cursor shape to be different from the default. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, Web[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_CUSTOM_CURSOR_SHAPE
values.summary: Display server supports setting the mouse cursor shape to a custom image. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Web[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_EMOJI_AND_SYMBOL_PICKER
values.summary: Display server supports system emoji and symbol picker. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_EXTEND_TO_TITLE
values.summary: Display server supports expanding window content to the title. See [constant WINDOW_FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE]. [b]macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_GLOBAL_MENU
values.summary: Display server supports global menu. This allows the application to display its menu items in the operating system's top bar. [b]macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_HDR_OUTPUT
values.summary: Display server supports HDR output. [b]Linux (Wayland), macOS, iOS, visionOS, Windows[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_HIDPI
values.summary: Display server supports querying the operating system's display scale factor. This allows automatically detecting the hiDPI display [i]reliably[/i], instead of guessing based on the screen resolution and the display's reported DPI (which might be unreliable due to broken monitor EDID). [b]Windows, Linux (Wayland), macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_ICON
values.summary: Display server supports changing the window icon (usually displayed in the top-left corner). [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b] [b]Note:[/b] Use on Wayland requires the compositor to implement the [url=https://wayland.app/protocols/xdg-toplevel-icon-v1#xdg_toplevel_icon_v1]xdg_toplevel_icon_v1[/url] protocol, which not all compositors do. See [url=https://wayland.app/protocols/xdg-toplevel-icon-v1#compositor-support]xdg_toplevel_icon_v1#compositor-support[/url] for more information on individual compositor support.

values.name: FEATURE_IME
values.summary: Display server supports [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Input_method]Input Method Editor[/url], which is commonly used for inputting Chinese/Japanese/Korean text. This is handled by the operating system, rather than by Godot. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11)[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_MOUSE
values.summary: Display server supports mouse input. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, Web[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_MOUSE_WARP
values.summary: Display server supports warping mouse coordinates to keep the mouse cursor constrained within an area, but looping when one of the edges is reached. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_COLOR_PICKER
values.summary: Display server supports native color picker. [b]Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG
values.summary: Display server supports spawning text dialogs using the operating system's native look-and-feel. See [method dialog_show]. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE
values.summary: Display server supports spawning dialogs for selecting files or directories using the operating system's native look-and-feel. See [method file_dialog_show]. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE_EXTRA
values.summary: The display server supports all features of [constant FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE], with the added functionality of Options and native dialog file access to [code]res://[/code] and [code]user://[/code] paths. See [method file_dialog_show] and [method file_dialog_with_options_show]. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE_MIME
values.summary: Native file selection dialog supports MIME types as filters.

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_INPUT
values.summary: Display server supports spawning text input dialogs using the operating system's native look-and-feel. See [method dialog_input_text]. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_HELP
values.summary: Display server supports native help system search callbacks. See [method help_set_search_callbacks].

values.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_ICON
values.summary: Display server supports changing the window icon (usually displayed in the top-left corner). [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_ORIENTATION
values.summary: Display server supports changing the screen orientation. [b]Android, iOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_PIP_MODE
values.summary: Display server supports putting the application in picture-in-picture mode. [b]Android[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_SCREEN_CAPTURE
values.summary: Display server supports reading screen pixels. See [method screen_get_pixel].

values.name: FEATURE_SCREEN_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE
values.summary: Display server supports [constant WINDOW_FLAG_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE] window flag. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_SELF_FITTING_WINDOWS
values.summary: Display server automatically fits popups according to the screen boundaries. Window nodes should not attempt to do that themselves.

values.name: FEATURE_STATUS_INDICATOR
values.summary: Display server supports application status indicators.

values.name: FEATURE_SUBWINDOWS
values.summary: Display server supports multiple windows that can be moved outside of the main window. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11)[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_SWAP_BUFFERS
values.summary: Display server supports V-Sync status can be changed from the default (which is forced to be enabled platforms not supporting this feature). [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_TEXT_TO_SPEECH
values.summary: Display server supports text-to-speech. See [code]tts_*[/code] methods. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, iOS, Web[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_TOUCHSCREEN
values.summary: Display server supports touchscreen input. [b]Windows, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, iOS, Web[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_VIRTUAL_KEYBOARD
values.summary: Display server supports popping up a virtual keyboard when requested to input text without a physical keyboard. [b]Android, iOS, Web[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_WINDOW_DRAG
values.summary: The display server supports initiating window drag and resize operations on demand. See [method window_start_drag] and [method window_start_resize].

values.name: FEATURE_WINDOW_EMBEDDING
values.summary: Display server supports embedding a window from another process. [b]Windows, Linux (X11), macOS[/b]

values.name: FEATURE_WINDOW_TRANSPARENCY
values.summary: Display server supports windows can use per-pixel transparency to make windows behind them partially or fully visible. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android[/b]

values.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_ANY
values.summary: The native file dialog allows selecting one file or directory.

values.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_DIR
values.summary: The native file dialog only allows selecting a directory, disallowing the selection of any file.

values.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_FILE
values.summary: The native file dialog allows selecting one, and only one file.

values.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_FILES
values.summary: The native file dialog allows selecting multiple files.

values.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_SAVE_FILE
values.summary: The native file dialog will warn when a file exists.

values.name: DISPLAY_HANDLE
values.summary: Display handle: - Linux (X11): [code]X11::Display*[/code] for the display. - Linux (Wayland): [code]wl_display[/code] for the display. - Android: [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the display.

values.name: EGL_CONFIG
values.summary: - Windows: [code]EGLConfig[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - macOS: [code]EGLConfig[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Linux (Wayland): [code]EGLConfig[/code] for the window.

values.name: EGL_DISPLAY
values.summary: - Windows: [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - macOS: [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Linux (Wayland): [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the window.

values.name: GLX_FBCONFIG
values.summary: The [code]GLXFBConfig[/code] for the window. [b]Note:[/b] Only available on Linux when using X11.

values.name: GLX_VISUALID
values.summary: The GLX [code]VisualID[/code] for the window. [b]Note:[/b] Only available on Linux when using X11.

values.name: OPENGL_CONTEXT
values.summary: OpenGL context (only with the Compatibility renderer): - Windows: [code]HGLRC[/code] for the window (native GL), or [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Linux (X11): [code]GLXContext*[/code] for the window. - Linux (Wayland): [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window. - macOS: [code]NSOpenGLContext*[/code] for the window (native GL), or [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Android: [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window.

values.name: WINDOW_HANDLE
values.summary: Window handle: - Windows: [code]HWND[/code] for the window. - Linux (X11): [code]X11::Window*[/code] for the window. - Linux (Wayland): [code]wl_surface[/code] for the window. - macOS: [code]NSWindow*[/code] for the window. - iOS: [code]UIViewController*[/code] for the view controller. - Android: [code]jObject[/code] for the activity.

values.name: WINDOW_VIEW
values.summary: Window view: - Windows: [code]HDC[/code] for the window (only with the Compatibility renderer). - macOS: [code]NSView*[/code] for the window main view. - iOS: [code]UIView*[/code] for the window main view.

values.name: MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED
values.summary: Captures the mouse. The mouse will be hidden and its position locked at the center of the window manager's window. [b]Note:[/b] If you want to process the mouse's movement in this mode, you need to use [member InputEventMouseMotion.relative].

values.name: MOUSE_MODE_CONFINED
values.summary: Confines the mouse cursor to the game window, and make it visible.

values.name: MOUSE_MODE_CONFINED_HIDDEN
values.summary: Confines the mouse cursor to the game window, and make it hidden.

values.name: MOUSE_MODE_HIDDEN
values.summary: Makes the mouse cursor hidden if it is visible.

values.name: MOUSE_MODE_MAX
values.summary: Max value of the [enum MouseMode].

values.name: MOUSE_MODE_VISIBLE
values.summary: Makes the mouse cursor visible if it is hidden.

values.name: PROGRESS_STATE_ERROR
values.summary: The progress indicator shows that an error has occurred. On Windows, the progress indicator turns red by default to show that an error has occurred in one of the windows that is broadcasting progress.

values.name: PROGRESS_STATE_INDETERMINATE
values.summary: The progress indicator shows an indeterminate progress. On Windows, the progress indicator does not grow in size, but cycles repeatedly along the length of the taskbar button by default.

values.name: PROGRESS_STATE_NOPROGRESS
values.summary: Stops displaying progress and returns the button to its normal state.

values.name: PROGRESS_STATE_NORMAL
values.summary: The progress indicator shows progress normally.

values.name: PROGRESS_STATE_PAUSED
values.summary: The progress indicator shows it was paused. On Windows, the progress indicator turns yellow by default to show that progress is currently stopped in one of the windows but can be resumed by the user.

values.name: SCREEN_LANDSCAPE
values.summary: Default landscape orientation.

values.name: SCREEN_PORTRAIT
values.summary: Default portrait orientation.

values.name: SCREEN_REVERSE_LANDSCAPE
values.summary: Reverse landscape orientation (upside down).

values.name: SCREEN_REVERSE_PORTRAIT
values.summary: Reverse portrait orientation (upside down).

values.name: SCREEN_SENSOR
values.summary: Automatic landscape or portrait orientation (default or reverse depending on sensor).

values.name: SCREEN_SENSOR_LANDSCAPE
values.summary: Automatic landscape orientation (default or reverse depending on sensor).

values.name: SCREEN_SENSOR_PORTRAIT
values.summary: Automatic portrait orientation (default or reverse depending on sensor).

values.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_BOUNDARY
values.summary: Utterance reached a word or sentence boundary.

values.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_CANCELED
values.summary: Utterance was canceled, or TTS service was unable to process it.

values.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_ENDED
values.summary: Utterance was successfully finished.

values.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_STARTED
values.summary: Utterance has begun to be spoken.

values.name: VSYNC_ADAPTIVE
values.summary: Behaves like [constant VSYNC_DISABLED] when the framerate drops below the screen's refresh rate to reduce stuttering (tearing may be visible). Otherwise, vertical synchronization is enabled to avoid tearing. Framerate is limited by the monitor refresh rate (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]). Behaves like [constant VSYNC_ENABLED] when using the Compatibility rendering method.

values.name: VSYNC_DISABLED
values.summary: No vertical synchronization, which means the engine will display frames as fast as possible (tearing may be visible). Framerate is unlimited (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]).

values.name: VSYNC_ENABLED
values.summary: Default vertical synchronization mode, the image is displayed only on vertical blanking intervals (no tearing is visible). Framerate is limited by the monitor refresh rate (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]).

values.name: VSYNC_MAILBOX
values.summary: Displays the most recent image in the queue on vertical blanking intervals, while rendering to the other images (no tearing is visible). Framerate is unlimited (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]). Although not guaranteed, the images can be rendered as fast as possible, which may reduce input lag (also called "Fast" V-Sync mode). [constant VSYNC_MAILBOX] works best when at least twice as many frames as the display refresh rate are rendered. Behaves like [constant VSYNC_ENABLED] when using the Compatibility rendering method.

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_DEFAULT
values.summary: Default text virtual keyboard.

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_EMAIL_ADDRESS
values.summary: Virtual keyboard with additional keys to assist with typing email addresses.

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_MULTILINE
values.summary: Multiline virtual keyboard.

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_NUMBER
values.summary: Virtual number keypad, useful for PIN entry.

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_NUMBER_DECIMAL
values.summary: Virtual number keypad, useful for entering fractional numbers.

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_PASSWORD
values.summary: Virtual keyboard for entering a password. On most platforms, this should disable autocomplete and autocapitalization. [b]Note:[/b] This is not supported on Web. Instead, this behaves identically to [constant KEYBOARD_TYPE_DEFAULT].

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_PHONE
values.summary: Virtual phone number keypad.

values.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_URL
values.summary: Virtual keyboard with additional keys to assist with typing URLs.

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_CLOSE_REQUEST
values.summary: Sent when the user has attempted to close the window (e.g. close button is pressed).

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_DPI_CHANGE
values.summary: Sent when the window is moved to the display with different DPI, or display DPI is changed. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS and Linux (Wayland).

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_FOCUS_IN
values.summary: Sent when the window grabs focus.

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_FOCUS_OUT
values.summary: Sent when the window loses focus.

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_FORCE_CLOSE
values.summary: Sent when the window has been forcibly closed by the display server. The window will immediately hide and clean any internal rendering references. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on Linux (Wayland).

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_GO_BACK_REQUEST
values.summary: Sent when the device "Back" button is pressed. [b]Note:[/b] This event is implemented only on Android.

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_MOUSE_ENTER
values.summary: Sent when the mouse pointer enters the window.

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_MOUSE_EXIT
values.summary: Sent when the mouse pointer exits the window.

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_OUTPUT_MAX_LINEAR_VALUE_CHANGED
values.summary: Sent when the output max linear value returned by [method Window.get_output_max_linear_value] has changed. This occurs when HDR output is enabled or disabled and when any HDR output luminance values of the window have changed, such as when the player adjusts their screen brightness setting or moves the window to a different screen.

values.name: WINDOW_EVENT_TITLEBAR_CHANGE
values.summary: Sent when the window title bar decoration is changed (e.g. [constant WINDOW_FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE] is set or window entered/exited full screen mode). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_ALWAYS_ON_TOP
values.summary: The window is floating on top of all other windows. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_BORDERLESS
values.summary: The window do not have native title bar and other decorations. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE
values.summary: Window is excluded from screenshots taken by [method screen_get_image], [method screen_get_image_rect], and [method screen_get_pixel]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on macOS and Windows (10, 20H1). [b]Note:[/b] Setting this flag will prevent standard screenshot methods from capturing a window image, but does [b]NOT[/b] guarantee that other apps won't be able to capture an image. It should not be used as a DRM or security measure.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE
values.summary: Window content is expanded to the full size of the window. Unlike borderless window, the frame is left intact and can be used to resize the window, title bar is transparent, but have minimize/maximize/close buttons. Use [method window_set_window_buttons_offset] to adjust minimize/maximize/close buttons offset. Use [method window_get_safe_title_margins] to determine area under the title bar that is not covered by decorations. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum WindowFlags] enum.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MAXIMIZE_DISABLED
values.summary: Window maximize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MINIMIZE_DISABLED
values.summary: Window minimize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MOUSE_PASSTHROUGH
values.summary: All mouse events are passed to the underlying window of the same application. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_NO_FOCUS
values.summary: The window can't be focused. No-focus window will ignore all input, except mouse clicks. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_POPUP
values.summary: Window is part of menu or [OptionButton] dropdown. This flag can't be changed when the window is visible. An active popup window will exclusively receive all input, without stealing focus from its parent. Popup windows are automatically closed when uses click outside it, or when an application is switched. Popup window must have transient parent set (see [method window_set_transient]). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_POPUP_WM_HINT
values.summary: Signals the window manager that this window is supposed to be an implementation-defined "popup" (usually a floating, borderless, untileable and immovable child window). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (Wayland).

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_RESIZE_DISABLED
values.summary: The window can't be resized by dragging its resize grip. It's still possible to resize the window using [method window_set_size]. This flag is ignored for full screen windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_SHARP_CORNERS
values.summary: Window style is overridden, forcing sharp corners. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on Windows (11).

values.name: WINDOW_FLAG_TRANSPARENT
values.summary: The window background can be transparent. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect if [method is_window_transparency_available] returns [code]false[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Transparency support is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, and Windows, but availability might vary depending on GPU driver, display manager, and compositor capabilities. [b]Note:[/b] Transparency support is implemented on Android, but can only be enabled via [member ProjectSettings.display/window/per_pixel_transparency/allowed]. This flag has no effect on Android.

values.name: WINDOW_MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN
values.summary: A single window full screen mode. This mode has less overhead, but only one window can be open on a given screen at a time (opening a child window or application switching will trigger a full screen transition). Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no border or decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]Note:[/b] This mode might not work with screen recording software. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On Windows:[/b] Depending on video driver, full screen transition might cause screens to go black for a moment. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. Exclusive full screen mode prevents Dock and Menu from showing up when the mouse pointer is hovering the edge of the screen. [b]On Linux (X11):[/b] Exclusive full screen mode bypasses compositor. [b]On Linux (Wayland):[/b] Equivalent to [constant WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN]. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

values.name: WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN
values.summary: Full screen mode with full multi-window support. Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

values.name: WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED
values.summary: Maximized window mode, i.e. [Window] will occupy whole screen area except task bar and still display its borders. Normally happens when the maximize button is pressed.

values.name: WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED
values.summary: Minimized window mode, i.e. [Window] is not visible and available on window manager's window list. Normally happens when the minimize button is pressed.

values.name: WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED
values.summary: Windowed mode, i.e. [Window] doesn't occupy the whole screen (unless set to the size of the screen).

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM
values.summary: Bottom edge of a window.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM_LEFT
values.summary: Bottom-left edge of a window.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM_RIGHT
values.summary: Bottom-right edge of a window.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_LEFT
values.summary: Left edge of a window.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum WindowResizeEdge] enum.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_RIGHT
values.summary: Right edge of a window.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_TOP
values.summary: Top edge of a window.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_TOP_LEFT
values.summary: Top-left edge of a window.

values.name: WINDOW_EDGE_TOP_RIGHT
values.summary: Top-right edge of a window.

constants.name: FEATURE_GLOBAL_MENU
constants.summary: Display server supports global menu. This allows the application to display its menu items in the operating system's top bar. [b]macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_SUBWINDOWS
constants.summary: Display server supports multiple windows that can be moved outside of the main window. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11)[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_TOUCHSCREEN
constants.summary: Display server supports touchscreen input. [b]Windows, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, iOS, Web[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_MOUSE
constants.summary: Display server supports mouse input. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, Web[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_MOUSE_WARP
constants.summary: Display server supports warping mouse coordinates to keep the mouse cursor constrained within an area, but looping when one of the edges is reached. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_CLIPBOARD
constants.summary: Display server supports setting and getting clipboard data. See also [constant FEATURE_CLIPBOARD_PRIMARY]. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, iOS, Web[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_VIRTUAL_KEYBOARD
constants.summary: Display server supports popping up a virtual keyboard when requested to input text without a physical keyboard. [b]Android, iOS, Web[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_CURSOR_SHAPE
constants.summary: Display server supports setting the mouse cursor shape to be different from the default. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, Web[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_CUSTOM_CURSOR_SHAPE
constants.summary: Display server supports setting the mouse cursor shape to a custom image. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Web[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG
constants.summary: Display server supports spawning text dialogs using the operating system's native look-and-feel. See [method dialog_show]. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_IME
constants.summary: Display server supports [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Input_method]Input Method Editor[/url], which is commonly used for inputting Chinese/Japanese/Korean text. This is handled by the operating system, rather than by Godot. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11)[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_WINDOW_TRANSPARENCY
constants.summary: Display server supports windows can use per-pixel transparency to make windows behind them partially or fully visible. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_HIDPI
constants.summary: Display server supports querying the operating system's display scale factor. This allows automatically detecting the hiDPI display [i]reliably[/i], instead of guessing based on the screen resolution and the display's reported DPI (which might be unreliable due to broken monitor EDID). [b]Windows, Linux (Wayland), macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_ICON
constants.summary: Display server supports changing the window icon (usually displayed in the top-left corner). [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b] [b]Note:[/b] Use on Wayland requires the compositor to implement the [url=https://wayland.app/protocols/xdg-toplevel-icon-v1#xdg_toplevel_icon_v1]xdg_toplevel_icon_v1[/url] protocol, which not all compositors do. See [url=https://wayland.app/protocols/xdg-toplevel-icon-v1#compositor-support]xdg_toplevel_icon_v1#compositor-support[/url] for more information on individual compositor support.

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_ICON
constants.summary: Display server supports changing the window icon (usually displayed in the top-left corner). [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_ORIENTATION
constants.summary: Display server supports changing the screen orientation. [b]Android, iOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_SWAP_BUFFERS
constants.summary: Display server supports V-Sync status can be changed from the default (which is forced to be enabled platforms not supporting this feature). [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_CLIPBOARD_PRIMARY
constants.summary: Display server supports Primary clipboard can be used. This is a different clipboard from [constant FEATURE_CLIPBOARD]. [b]Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_TEXT_TO_SPEECH
constants.summary: Display server supports text-to-speech. See [code]tts_*[/code] methods. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android, iOS, Web[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_EXTEND_TO_TITLE
constants.summary: Display server supports expanding window content to the title. See [constant WINDOW_FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE]. [b]macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_SCREEN_CAPTURE
constants.summary: Display server supports reading screen pixels. See [method screen_get_pixel].

constants.name: FEATURE_STATUS_INDICATOR
constants.summary: Display server supports application status indicators.

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_HELP
constants.summary: Display server supports native help system search callbacks. See [method help_set_search_callbacks].

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_INPUT
constants.summary: Display server supports spawning text input dialogs using the operating system's native look-and-feel. See [method dialog_input_text]. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE
constants.summary: Display server supports spawning dialogs for selecting files or directories using the operating system's native look-and-feel. See [method file_dialog_show]. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland), Android[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE_EXTRA
constants.summary: The display server supports all features of [constant FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE], with the added functionality of Options and native dialog file access to [code]res://[/code] and [code]user://[/code] paths. See [method file_dialog_show] and [method file_dialog_with_options_show]. [b]Windows, macOS, Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_WINDOW_DRAG
constants.summary: The display server supports initiating window drag and resize operations on demand. See [method window_start_drag] and [method window_start_resize].

constants.name: FEATURE_SCREEN_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE
constants.summary: Display server supports [constant WINDOW_FLAG_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE] window flag. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_WINDOW_EMBEDDING
constants.summary: Display server supports embedding a window from another process. [b]Windows, Linux (X11), macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_DIALOG_FILE_MIME
constants.summary: Native file selection dialog supports MIME types as filters.

constants.name: FEATURE_EMOJI_AND_SYMBOL_PICKER
constants.summary: Display server supports system emoji and symbol picker. [b]Windows, macOS[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_NATIVE_COLOR_PICKER
constants.summary: Display server supports native color picker. [b]Linux (X11/Wayland)[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_SELF_FITTING_WINDOWS
constants.summary: Display server automatically fits popups according to the screen boundaries. Window nodes should not attempt to do that themselves.

constants.name: FEATURE_ACCESSIBILITY_SCREEN_READER
constants.summary: Display server supports interaction with screen reader or Braille display. [b]Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, Windows[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_HDR_OUTPUT
constants.summary: Display server supports HDR output. [b]Linux (Wayland), macOS, iOS, visionOS, Windows[/b]

constants.name: FEATURE_PIP_MODE
constants.summary: Display server supports putting the application in picture-in-picture mode. [b]Android[/b]

constants.name: SCREEN_WITH_KEYBOARD_FOCUS
constants.summary: Represents the screen containing the window with the keyboard focus. [b]Note:[/b] On Android, iOS, Web, and Linux (Wayland), this constant always represents the screen at index [code]0[/code].

constants.name: SCREEN_PRIMARY

constants.name: SCREEN_OF_MAIN_WINDOW
constants.summary: Represents the screen where the main window is located. This is usually the default value in functions that allow specifying one of several screens. [b]Note:[/b] On Android, iOS, Web, and Linux (Wayland), this constant always represents the screen at index [code]0[/code].

constants.name: INVALID_WINDOW_ID

constants.summary: Default text virtual keyboard.

constants.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_MULTILINE
constants.summary: Multiline virtual keyboard.

constants.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_NUMBER
constants.summary: Virtual number keypad, useful for PIN entry.

constants.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_NUMBER_DECIMAL
constants.summary: Virtual number keypad, useful for entering fractional numbers.

constants.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_PHONE
constants.summary: Virtual phone number keypad.

constants.summary: Virtual keyboard with additional keys to assist with typing email addresses.

constants.summary: Virtual keyboard for entering a password. On most platforms, this should disable autocomplete and autocapitalization. [b]Note:[/b] This is not supported on Web. Instead, this behaves identically to [constant KEYBOARD_TYPE_DEFAULT].

constants.name: KEYBOARD_TYPE_URL
constants.summary: Virtual keyboard with additional keys to assist with typing URLs.

constants.name: CURSOR_ARROW
constants.summary: Arrow cursor shape. This is the default when not pointing anything that overrides the mouse cursor, such as a [LineEdit] or [TextEdit].

constants.name: CURSOR_IBEAM
constants.summary: I-beam cursor shape. This is used by default when hovering a control that accepts text input, such as [LineEdit] or [TextEdit].

constants.name: CURSOR_POINTING_HAND
constants.summary: Pointing hand cursor shape. This is used by default when hovering a [LinkButton] or a URL tag in a [RichTextLabel].

constants.name: CURSOR_CROSS
constants.summary: Crosshair cursor. This is intended to be displayed when the user needs precise aim over an element, such as a rectangle selection tool or a color picker.

constants.name: CURSOR_WAIT
constants.summary: Wait cursor. On most cursor themes, this displays a spinning icon [i]besides[/i] the arrow. Intended to be used for non-blocking operations (when the user can do something else at the moment). See also [constant CURSOR_BUSY].

constants.name: CURSOR_BUSY
constants.summary: Wait cursor. On most cursor themes, this [i]replaces[/i] the arrow with a spinning icon. Intended to be used for blocking operations (when the user can't do anything else at the moment). See also [constant CURSOR_WAIT].

constants.name: CURSOR_DRAG
constants.summary: Dragging hand cursor. This is displayed during drag-and-drop operations. See also [constant CURSOR_CAN_DROP].

constants.name: CURSOR_CAN_DROP
constants.summary: "Can drop" cursor. This is displayed during drag-and-drop operations if hovering over a [Control] that can accept the drag-and-drop event. On most cursor themes, this displays a dragging hand with an arrow symbol besides it. See also [constant CURSOR_DRAG].

constants.name: CURSOR_FORBIDDEN
constants.summary: Forbidden cursor. This is displayed during drag-and-drop operations if the hovered [Control] can't accept the drag-and-drop event.

constants.name: CURSOR_VSIZE
constants.summary: Vertical resize cursor. Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be vertically resized using the mouse. See also [constant CURSOR_VSPLIT].

constants.name: CURSOR_HSIZE
constants.summary: Horizontal resize cursor. Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be horizontally resized using the mouse. See also [constant CURSOR_HSPLIT].

constants.name: CURSOR_BDIAGSIZE
constants.summary: Secondary diagonal resize cursor (top-right/bottom-left). Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be resized on both axes at once using the mouse.

constants.name: CURSOR_FDIAGSIZE
constants.summary: Main diagonal resize cursor (top-left/bottom-right). Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be resized on both axes at once using the mouse.

constants.name: CURSOR_MOVE
constants.summary: Move cursor. Intended to be displayed when the hovered [Control] can be moved using the mouse.

constants.name: CURSOR_VSPLIT
constants.summary: Vertical split cursor. This is displayed when hovering a [Control] with splits that can be vertically resized using the mouse, such as [VSplitContainer]. On some cursor themes, this cursor may have the same appearance as [constant CURSOR_VSIZE].

constants.name: CURSOR_HSPLIT
constants.summary: Horizontal split cursor. This is displayed when hovering a [Control] with splits that can be horizontally resized using the mouse, such as [HSplitContainer]. On some cursor themes, this cursor may have the same appearance as [constant CURSOR_HSIZE].

constants.name: CURSOR_HELP
constants.summary: Help cursor. On most cursor themes, this displays a question mark icon instead of the mouse cursor. Intended to be used when the user has requested help on the next element that will be clicked.

constants.name: CURSOR_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum CursorShape] enum.

constants.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_FILE
constants.summary: The native file dialog allows selecting one, and only one file.

constants.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_FILES
constants.summary: The native file dialog allows selecting multiple files.

constants.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_DIR
constants.summary: The native file dialog only allows selecting a directory, disallowing the selection of any file.

constants.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_OPEN_ANY
constants.summary: The native file dialog allows selecting one file or directory.

constants.name: FILE_DIALOG_MODE_SAVE_FILE
constants.summary: The native file dialog will warn when a file exists.

constants.name: WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED
constants.summary: Windowed mode, i.e. [Window] doesn't occupy the whole screen (unless set to the size of the screen).

constants.name: WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED
constants.summary: Minimized window mode, i.e. [Window] is not visible and available on window manager's window list. Normally happens when the minimize button is pressed.

constants.name: WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED
constants.summary: Maximized window mode, i.e. [Window] will occupy whole screen area except task bar and still display its borders. Normally happens when the maximize button is pressed.

constants.name: WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN
constants.summary: Full screen mode with full multi-window support. Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

constants.name: WINDOW_MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN
constants.summary: A single window full screen mode. This mode has less overhead, but only one window can be open on a given screen at a time (opening a child window or application switching will trigger a full screen transition). Full screen window covers the entire display area of a screen and has no border or decorations. The display's video mode is not changed. [b]Note:[/b] This mode might not work with screen recording software. [b]On Android:[/b] This enables immersive mode. [b]On Windows:[/b] Depending on video driver, full screen transition might cause screens to go black for a moment. [b]On macOS:[/b] A new desktop is used to display the running project. Exclusive full screen mode prevents Dock and Menu from showing up when the mouse pointer is hovering the edge of the screen. [b]On Linux (X11):[/b] Exclusive full screen mode bypasses compositor. [b]On Linux (Wayland):[/b] Equivalent to [constant WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN]. [b]Note:[/b] Regardless of the platform, enabling full screen will change the window size to match the monitor's size. Therefore, make sure your project supports [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/rendering/multiple_resolutions.html]multiple resolutions[/url] when enabling full screen mode.

constants.name: PROGRESS_STATE_NOPROGRESS
constants.summary: Stops displaying progress and returns the button to its normal state.

constants.name: PROGRESS_STATE_INDETERMINATE
constants.summary: The progress indicator shows an indeterminate progress. On Windows, the progress indicator does not grow in size, but cycles repeatedly along the length of the taskbar button by default.

constants.name: PROGRESS_STATE_NORMAL
constants.summary: The progress indicator shows progress normally.

constants.name: PROGRESS_STATE_ERROR
constants.summary: The progress indicator shows that an error has occurred. On Windows, the progress indicator turns red by default to show that an error has occurred in one of the windows that is broadcasting progress.

constants.name: PROGRESS_STATE_PAUSED
constants.summary: The progress indicator shows it was paused. On Windows, the progress indicator turns yellow by default to show that progress is currently stopped in one of the windows but can be resumed by the user.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_RESIZE_DISABLED
constants.summary: The window can't be resized by dragging its resize grip. It's still possible to resize the window using [method window_set_size]. This flag is ignored for full screen windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_BORDERLESS
constants.summary: The window do not have native title bar and other decorations. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_ALWAYS_ON_TOP
constants.summary: The window is floating on top of all other windows. This flag is ignored for full-screen windows. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_TRANSPARENT
constants.summary: The window background can be transparent. [b]Note:[/b] This flag has no effect if [method is_window_transparency_available] returns [code]false[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Transparency support is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, and Windows, but availability might vary depending on GPU driver, display manager, and compositor capabilities. [b]Note:[/b] Transparency support is implemented on Android, but can only be enabled via [member ProjectSettings.display/window/per_pixel_transparency/allowed]. This flag has no effect on Android.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_NO_FOCUS
constants.summary: The window can't be focused. No-focus window will ignore all input, except mouse clicks. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_POPUP
constants.summary: Window is part of menu or [OptionButton] dropdown. This flag can't be changed when the window is visible. An active popup window will exclusively receive all input, without stealing focus from its parent. Popup windows are automatically closed when uses click outside it, or when an application is switched. Popup window must have transient parent set (see [method window_set_transient]). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11/Wayland), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE
constants.summary: Window content is expanded to the full size of the window. Unlike borderless window, the frame is left intact and can be used to resize the window, title bar is transparent, but have minimize/maximize/close buttons. Use [method window_set_window_buttons_offset] to adjust minimize/maximize/close buttons offset. Use [method window_get_safe_title_margins] to determine area under the title bar that is not covered by decorations. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MOUSE_PASSTHROUGH
constants.summary: All mouse events are passed to the underlying window of the same application. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_SHARP_CORNERS
constants.summary: Window style is overridden, forcing sharp corners. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on Windows (11).

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_EXCLUDE_FROM_CAPTURE
constants.summary: Window is excluded from screenshots taken by [method screen_get_image], [method screen_get_image_rect], and [method screen_get_pixel]. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on macOS and Windows (10, 20H1). [b]Note:[/b] Setting this flag will prevent standard screenshot methods from capturing a window image, but does [b]NOT[/b] guarantee that other apps won't be able to capture an image. It should not be used as a DRM or security measure.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_POPUP_WM_HINT
constants.summary: Signals the window manager that this window is supposed to be an implementation-defined "popup" (usually a floating, borderless, untileable and immovable child window). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (Wayland).

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MINIMIZE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Window minimize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MAXIMIZE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Window maximize button is disabled. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented on Linux (X11), macOS, and Windows.

constants.name: WINDOW_FLAG_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum WindowFlags] enum.

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_MOUSE_ENTER
constants.summary: Sent when the mouse pointer enters the window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_MOUSE_EXIT
constants.summary: Sent when the mouse pointer exits the window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_FOCUS_IN
constants.summary: Sent when the window grabs focus.

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_FOCUS_OUT
constants.summary: Sent when the window loses focus.

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_CLOSE_REQUEST
constants.summary: Sent when the user has attempted to close the window (e.g. close button is pressed).

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_GO_BACK_REQUEST
constants.summary: Sent when the device "Back" button is pressed. [b]Note:[/b] This event is implemented only on Android.

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_DPI_CHANGE
constants.summary: Sent when the window is moved to the display with different DPI, or display DPI is changed. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS and Linux (Wayland).

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_TITLEBAR_CHANGE
constants.summary: Sent when the window title bar decoration is changed (e.g. [constant WINDOW_FLAG_EXTEND_TO_TITLE] is set or window entered/exited full screen mode). [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on macOS.

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_FORCE_CLOSE
constants.summary: Sent when the window has been forcibly closed by the display server. The window will immediately hide and clean any internal rendering references. [b]Note:[/b] This flag is implemented only on Linux (Wayland).

constants.name: WINDOW_EVENT_OUTPUT_MAX_LINEAR_VALUE_CHANGED
constants.summary: Sent when the output max linear value returned by [method Window.get_output_max_linear_value] has changed. This occurs when HDR output is enabled or disabled and when any HDR output luminance values of the window have changed, such as when the player adjusts their screen brightness setting or moves the window to a different screen.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_TOP_LEFT
constants.summary: Top-left edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_TOP
constants.summary: Top edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_TOP_RIGHT
constants.summary: Top-right edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_LEFT
constants.summary: Left edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_RIGHT
constants.summary: Right edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM_LEFT
constants.summary: Bottom-left edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Bottom edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM_RIGHT
constants.summary: Bottom-right edge of a window.

constants.name: WINDOW_EDGE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum WindowResizeEdge] enum.

constants.name: VSYNC_DISABLED
constants.summary: No vertical synchronization, which means the engine will display frames as fast as possible (tearing may be visible). Framerate is unlimited (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]).

constants.name: VSYNC_ENABLED
constants.summary: Default vertical synchronization mode, the image is displayed only on vertical blanking intervals (no tearing is visible). Framerate is limited by the monitor refresh rate (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]).

constants.name: VSYNC_ADAPTIVE
constants.summary: Behaves like [constant VSYNC_DISABLED] when the framerate drops below the screen's refresh rate to reduce stuttering (tearing may be visible). Otherwise, vertical synchronization is enabled to avoid tearing. Framerate is limited by the monitor refresh rate (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]). Behaves like [constant VSYNC_ENABLED] when using the Compatibility rendering method.

constants.name: VSYNC_MAILBOX
constants.summary: Displays the most recent image in the queue on vertical blanking intervals, while rendering to the other images (no tearing is visible). Framerate is unlimited (regardless of [member Engine.max_fps]). Although not guaranteed, the images can be rendered as fast as possible, which may reduce input lag (also called "Fast" V-Sync mode). [constant VSYNC_MAILBOX] works best when at least twice as many frames as the display refresh rate are rendered. Behaves like [constant VSYNC_ENABLED] when using the Compatibility rendering method.

constants.name: DISPLAY_HANDLE
constants.summary: Display handle: - Linux (X11): [code]X11::Display*[/code] for the display. - Linux (Wayland): [code]wl_display[/code] for the display. - Android: [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the display.

constants.name: WINDOW_HANDLE
constants.summary: Window handle: - Windows: [code]HWND[/code] for the window. - Linux (X11): [code]X11::Window*[/code] for the window. - Linux (Wayland): [code]wl_surface[/code] for the window. - macOS: [code]NSWindow*[/code] for the window. - iOS: [code]UIViewController*[/code] for the view controller. - Android: [code]jObject[/code] for the activity.

constants.name: WINDOW_VIEW
constants.summary: Window view: - Windows: [code]HDC[/code] for the window (only with the Compatibility renderer). - macOS: [code]NSView*[/code] for the window main view. - iOS: [code]UIView*[/code] for the window main view.

constants.name: OPENGL_CONTEXT
constants.summary: OpenGL context (only with the Compatibility renderer): - Windows: [code]HGLRC[/code] for the window (native GL), or [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Linux (X11): [code]GLXContext*[/code] for the window. - Linux (Wayland): [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window. - macOS: [code]NSOpenGLContext*[/code] for the window (native GL), or [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Android: [code]EGLContext[/code] for the window.

constants.name: EGL_DISPLAY
constants.summary: - Windows: [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - macOS: [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Linux (Wayland): [code]EGLDisplay[/code] for the window.

constants.name: EGL_CONFIG
constants.summary: - Windows: [code]EGLConfig[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - macOS: [code]EGLConfig[/code] for the window (ANGLE). - Linux (Wayland): [code]EGLConfig[/code] for the window.

constants.name: GLX_VISUALID
constants.summary: The GLX [code]VisualID[/code] for the window. [b]Note:[/b] Only available on Linux when using X11.

constants.name: GLX_FBCONFIG
constants.summary: The [code]GLXFBConfig[/code] for the window. [b]Note:[/b] Only available on Linux when using X11.

constants.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_STARTED
constants.summary: Utterance has begun to be spoken.

constants.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_ENDED
constants.summary: Utterance was successfully finished.

constants.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_CANCELED
constants.summary: Utterance was canceled, or TTS service was unable to process it.

constants.name: TTS_UTTERANCE_BOUNDARY
constants.summary: Utterance reached a word or sentence boundary.
